label: saiawosh | come | day
sentence:
9 - Saiawosh

On a certain day it came about that Toos, Gew, Goodarz, and other brave knights of Iran
went forth to chase wild asses in the forests of Daghoui.

label: they | find | woman
sentence:
Now when they were come into
the wood, they found therein a woman of surpassing beauty, and the hearts of Toos and
Geew burned towards her in love.

label: each | desire | ∅
sentence:
And when they had questioned her of her lineage, and
learned that she was of the race of Feridoun, each desired to take her to wife.

label: none | give | way
sentence:
But none
would give way unto the other, and hot words were bandied, and they were like to come
unto blows.

label: ∅ | let | ∅
sentence:
Then one spake, and said-
"I counsel you, let Kai Kawous decide between you."

label: they | listen | voice
sentence:
And they listened to the voice of
the counsellor, and they took with them the Peri-faced, and led her before Kai Kawous,
and recounted to him all that was come about.

label: kawous | long | she
sentence:
But Kai Kawous, when he beheld the
beauty of the maid, longed after her for himself, and he said that she was worthy of the
throne; and he took her and led her into the house of his women.

label: ∅ | bear | day
sentence:
Now after many days there was born to her a son, and he was of goodly mien, tall and
strong, and the name that was given to him was Saiawosh.

label: kawous | rejoice | son
sentence:
And Kai Kawous rejoiced in
this son of his race, but he was grieved also because of the message of the stars
concerning him.

label: neither | avail | he
sentence:
For it was written that the heavens were hostile unto this infant; neither
would his virtues avail him aught, for these above all would lead him into destruction.

label: news | spread | meantime
sentence:
In the meantime the news that a son had been born unto the Shah spread even unto the
land of Rostam.

label: pehliva | arouse | he
sentence:
And the Pehliva, when he learned thereof, aroused him from his sorrow
for Sohrab, and he came forth out of Zaboulistan, and asked for the babe at the hands of
its father, that he might rear it unto Iran.

label: kawous | suffer | it
sentence:
And Kai Kawous suffered it, and Rostam bare
the child unto his kingdom, and trained him in the arts of war and of the banquet.

label: saiawosh | increase | might
sentence:
And
Saiawosh increased in might and beauty, and you would have said that the world held not
his like.

label: he | come | rostam
sentence:
Now when Saiawosh was become strong (so that he could ensnare a lion), he came before

Rostam, bearing high his head.

label: he | spake | ∅
sentence:
And he spake, saying-
"I desire to go before the Shah, that my father may behold me, and see what manner of
man thou hast made of me."

label: rostam | deem | ∅
sentence: And Rostam deemed that he spake well.

label: he | make | preparation
sentence:
So he made great preparations, and marched
unto Iran with a mighty host, and Saiawosh rode with him at their head.

label: land | rejoice | ∅
sentence:
And the land
rejoiced when it looked on the face of Saiawosh, and there was great joy in the courts of
the King, and jewels and gold and precious things past the telling rained upon Rostam
and Saiawosh his charge.

label: kawous | be | ∅
sentence:
And Kai Kawous was glad when he beheld the boy, and gave
rich rewards unto Rostam; but Saiawosh did he place beside him on the throne.

label: man | spake | praise
sentence:
And all
men spake his praises, and there was a feast given, such as the world hath not seen the
like.

label: saiawosh | stay | court
sentence:
Then Saiawosh stayed in the courts of his father, and seven years did he prove his spirit;
but in the eighth, when he had found him worthy, he gave unto him a throne and a crown.

label: all | be | ∅
sentence: And all was well, and men had forgotten the evil message of the stars.

label: it | accomplish | ∅
sentence:
But that which is
written in the heavens, it is surely accomplished, and the day of ill fortune drew nigh.

label: it | come | sudaveh
sentence:
For
it came about that Sudaveh beheld the youth of Saiawosh, and her eyes were filled with
his beauty, and her soul burned after him.

label: she | send | messenger
sentence:
So she sent unto him a messenger, and invited
him to enter the house of the women.

label: he | send | word
sentence:
But he sent in answer words of excuse, for he
trusted her not.

label: sudaveh | make | complaint
sentence:
Then Sudaveh made complaint before Kai Kawous that Saiawosh had
deafened his ear unto her request, and she bade the Shah send him behind the curtains of
the women's house, that his son might become acquainted with his sisters.

label: kawous | do | that
sentence:
And Kai
Kawous did that which Sudaveh asked of him, and Saiawosh obeyed his commands.

label: sudaveh | desire | ∅
sentence:
But Sudaveh, when she had so far accomplished her longing that she had gotten him
within the house, desired that he should speak with her alone.

label: saiawosh | resist | wish
sentence:
But Saiawosh resisted her
wish.

label: sudaveh | entice | he
sentence:
And three times did Sudaveh entice him behind the curtains of the house, and three
times was Saiawosh cold unto her yearning.

label: sudaveh | be | wroth
sentence:
Then Sudaveh was wroth, and she made
complaint unto the Shah, and she slandered the fair fame of Saiawosh, and she spread
evil reports of him throughout the land, and she inflamed the heart of Kai Kawous against
his son.

label: shah | anger | measure
sentence:
Now the Shah was angered beyond measure, and it availed nought unto Saiawosh
to defend himself, for Kai Kawous was filled with the love of Sudaveh, and he listened
only unto her voice.

label: he | remember | ∅
sentence:
And he remembered how she had borne his captivity in Hamaveran,
and he knew not of her evil deceits.

label: kawous | trouble | spirit
sentence:
And when she said that Saiawosh had done her great
wrong, Kai Kawous was troubled in his spirit, and he resolved how he should act, for his
heart went out also unto his son, and he feared that guile lurked in these things.

label: he | decide | they
sentence:
And he
could not decide between them.

label: he | cause | ∅
sentence:
So he caused dromedaries to be sent forth, even unto the
borders of the land, and bring forth wood from the forests.

label: they | do | ∅
sentence:
And they did so, and there was
reared a mighty heap of logs, so that the eye could behold it at a distance of two farsangs.

label: it | pile | ∅
sentence:
And it was piled so that a path ran through its midst such as a mounted knight could
traverse.

label: great | be | width
sentence:
And the Shah commanded that naphtha be poured upon the wood; and when it
was done he bade that it be lighted, and there were needed two hundred men to light the
pyre, so great was its width and height.

label: flame | shout | fear
sentence:
And the flames and smoke overspread the
heavens, and men shouted for fear when they beheld the tongues of fire, and the heat
thereof was felt in the far corners of the land.

label: saiawosh | ride | midst
sentence:
Now when all was ready, Kai Kawous bade Saiawosh his son ride into the midst of the
burning mount, that he might prove his innocence.

label: saiawosh | do | ∅
sentence:
And Saiawosh did as the King
commanded, and he came before Kai Kawous, and saluted him, and made him ready for
the ordeal.

label: he | commend | soul
sentence:
And when he came nigh unto the burning wood, he commended his soul unto
God, and prayed that He would make him pure before his father.

label: he | give | rein
sentence:
And when he had done
so, he gave rein unto his horse, and entered into the flame.

label: cry | arise | man
sentence:
And a great cry of sorrow
arose from all men in the plains and in the city, for they held that no man could come
forth alive from this furnace.

label: sudaveh | hear | cry
sentence:
And Sudaveh heard the cry, and came forth upon the roof of
her house that she might behold the sight, and she prayed that ill might befall unto
Saiawosh, and she held her eyes fastened upon the pyre.

label: noble | gaze | face
sentence:
But the nobles gazed on the face
of Kai Kawous, and their mouths were filled with execrations, and their lips trembled
with wrath at this deed.

label: saiawosh | ride | undaunted
sentence:
And Saiawosh rode on undaunted, and his white robes and ebon steed shone forth
between the flames, and their anger was reflected upon his helmet of gold.

label: he | ride | ∅
sentence:
And he rode
until he was come unto the end of the pathway, and when he came forth there was not
singed a hair of his head, neither had the smoke blackened his garments.

label: they | rent | welkin
sentence:
Now when the people beheld that he was come forth alive, they rent the welkin with their
shouting.

label: ∅ | be | joy
sentence:
And the nobles came forth to greet him, and, save only Sudaveh, there was joy
in all hearts.

label: saiawosh | ride | ∅
sentence:
Now Saiawosh rode till he came before the Shah, and then he got him off his
horse, and did homage before his father.

label: he | know | ∅
sentence:
And when Kai Kawous beheld him, and saw that
there were no signs of fire about him, he knew that he was innocent.

label: he | raise | son
sentence:
So he raised his son
from off the ground, and placed Saiawosh beside him on the throne, and asked his
forgiveness for that which was come to pass.

label: saiawosh | grant | it
sentence: And Saiawosh granted it.

label: kawous | feast | son
sentence:
Then Kai Kawous
feasted his son with wine and song, and three days did they spend in revels, and the door
of the King's treasury was opened.

label: kawous | mount | throne
sentence: But on the fourth day Kai Kawous mounted the throne of the Kaianides.

label: he | take | hand
sentence:
He took in his
hand the ox-headed mace, and he commanded that Sudaveh be led before him.

label: he | reproach | she
sentence:
Then he
reproached her with her evil deeds, and he bade her make ready to depart the world, for
verily death was decreed unto her.

label: sudaveh | ask | vain
sentence:
And in vain did Sudaveh ask for pardon at the hands
of the King, for she continued to speak ill of Saiawosh, and she said that by the arts of
magic alone had he escaped the fire, and she ceased not to cry against him.

label: king | give | order
sentence:
So the King
gave orders that she be led forth unto death, and the nobles approved his resolve, and
invoked the blessings of Heaven upon the head of the Shah.

label: ∅ | grieve | ∅
sentence:
But Saiawosh, when he
learned it, was grieved, for he knew that the woman was beloved of his father.

label: he | go | kawous
sentence:
And he
went before Kai Kawous, and craved her pardon.

label: kawous | grant | it
sentence:
And Kai Kawous granted it with
gladness, for his heart yearned after Sudaveh.

label: saiawosh | lead | she
sentence:
So Saiawosh led her back, and the curtains
of the house of the women hid her once more behind them, and the Shah was glad again
in her sight.

label: it | come | ∅
sentence:
Then it came about that the love of Kai Kawous for Sudaveh grew yet mightier, and he
was as wax under her hands.

label: she | fill | ear
sentence:
And when she saw that her empire over him was

strengthened, she filled his ear with plaints of Saiawosh, and she darkened the mind of
the Shah till that his spirit was troubled, and he knew not where he should turn for truth.

label: afrasiyab | make | ∅
sentence:
Now while Kai Kawous thus dallied behind the curtains of his house, Afrasiyab made
him ready with three thousand chosen men to fall upon the land of Iran.

label: kawous | be | ∅
sentence:
And Kai Kawous,
when he learned it, was sad, for he knew that he must exchange the banquet for the battle;
and he was angered also with Afrasiyab, and he poured out words of reproof against him
because he had broken his covenant and had once more attacked his land.

label: he | make | ∅
sentence:
Yet he made
him ready to lead forth his army.

label: mubid | pray | he
sentence:
Then a Mubid prayed him that he would not go forth
himself, and he recalled unto Kai Kawous how twice already he had endangered his
kingdom.

label: kawous | be | wroth
sentence:
But Kai Kawous was wroth when he heard these words, and he bade the Mubid
depart from his presence, and he sware that he alone could turn the army unto good issue.

label: saiawosh | take | heart
sentence:
But Saiawosh, when he heard it, took heart of grace, for he thought within his spirit, "If
the King grant unto me to lead forth his army, perchance I may win unto myself a name
of valour, and be delivered from the wiles of Sudaveh."

label: he | gird | himself
sentence:
So he girded himself with the
armour of battle and came before the King his father, and made known to him his request.

label: he | recall | kawous
sentence:
And he recalled unto Kai Kawous how that he was his son, and how he was sprung from
a worthy race, and how his rank permitted him to lead forth a host; and Kai Kawous
listened to his words with gladness, and assented to his desires.

label: messenger | send | rostam
sentence:
Then messengers were
sent unto Rostam to bid him go forth to battle with his charge and guard him.

label: kawous | say | pehliva-
sentence:
And Kai
Kawous said unto his Pehliva-
"If thou watchest over him, I can slumber; but if thou reposest, then doth it beseem me to
act.

label: rostam | answer | ∅
sentence:
"
And Rostam answered and said, "O King, I am thy servant, and it behoveth me to do thy
will.

label: i | rejoice | ∅
sentence:
As for Saiawosh, he is the light of my heart and the joy of my soul; I rejoice to lead
him forth before his enemies.

label: trumpet | sound | ∅
sentence:
"
So the trumpets of war were sounded, and the clang of armour and the tramp of horsemen
and of foot filled the air.

label: they | pass | order
sentence:
And they passed in order before Kai Kawous, and he blessed the
troops and his son, who rode at their head.

label: he | spake | ∅
sentence:
And he spake, saying-
"May thy good star shine down upon thee, and mayst thou come back to me victorious
and glad.

label: kawous | return | he
sentence:
"
Then Kai Kawous returned him unto his house, and Saiawosh gave the signal to depart.

label: they | march | ∅
sentence: And they marched until they came unto the land of Zaboulistan.

label: they | rest | they
sentence: Now when they were come there they rested them a while, and feasted in the house of Zal.

label: ∅ | come | ∅
sentence:
And while they revelled there came out to join them riders from Cabul and from Ind, and
wherever there was a king of might he sent over his army to aid them.

label: they | take | leave
sentence:
Then when a
month had rolled above their heads they took their leave of Zal and of Zaboulistan, and
went forward till they came unto Balkh.

label: man | meet | they
sentence:
And at Balkh the men of Turan met them, and
Gersiwaz, the brother of Afrasiyab, was at their head.

label: he | know | ∅
sentence:
Now when he saw the hosts of Iran,
he knew that the hour to fight was come.

label: army | make | they
sentence:
So the two armies made them in order, and they
waged battle hot and sore, and for three days the fighting raged without ceasing, but on
the fourth victory passed over to Iran.

label: saiawosh | call | scribe
sentence:
Then Saiawosh called before him a scribe, and
wrote a letter, perfumed with musk, unto Kai Kawous his father.

label: he | tell | he
sentence:
And when he had
invoked the blessings of Heaven upon his head, he told him all that was come to pass,
and how he had conquered the foes of Iran.

label: kawous | rejoice | ∅
sentence:
And Kai Kawous, when he had read the letter,
rejoiced, and wrote an answer unto his son, and his gladness shone in his words, and you
would have said it was a letter like to the tender green of spring.

label: afrasiyab | be | ∅
sentence:
But Afrasiyab, when he learned the news, was discomfited, and that which Gersiwaz told
unto him was bitter to his taste, and he was beside himself for anger.

label: afrasiyab | lay | he
sentence:
Now when he had
heard his brother to an end, Afrasiyab laid him down to slumber.

label: night | spend | ∅
sentence:
Yet ere the night was
spent there came out one to the house of Gersiwaz and told unto him that Afrasiyab was
shouting like to a man bereft of reason.

label: gersiwaz | go | king
sentence:
Then Gersiwaz went in unto the King, and he
beheld him lying upon the floor of his chamber roaring in agony of spirit.

label: he | raise | he
sentence:
Then he raised
him, and questioned him wherefore he cried out thus.

label: he | desire | ∅
sentence:
"
Then he desired that torches be brought within to light up the darkness, and he gathered
his robes about him and mounted upon his throne.

label: he | call | mubids
sentence:
And when he had done so he called for
the Mubids, and he recounted to them the dream that had visited his slumber.

label: he | tell | ∅
sentence:
And he told
how that he had seen the earth filled with serpents, and the Iranians were fallen upon him,
and evil was come to him from Kai Kawous and a boy that stood beside him on the
throne.

label: he | tremble | ∅
sentence:
And he trembled as he related his dream, and he would take no comfort from the
words of Gersiwaz.

label: they | dare | fear
sentence:
Now the Mubids as they listened were afraid, and when Afrasiyab bade them open their
lips, they dared not for fear.

label: king | say | ∅
sentence:
Then the King said that he would cleave open their heads if
they spake not, and he sware unto them a great oath that he would spare them, even
though the words they should utter be evil.

label: they | reveal | he
sentence:
Then they revealed to him how it was written
that Saiawosh would bring destruction upon Turan, and how he would be victorious over
the Turks, and how, even though he should fall by the hands of Afrasiyab, this evil could
not be stayed.

label: they | counsel | afrasiyab
sentence:
And they counselled Afrasiyab to contend no longer against the son of Kai
Kawous, for surely if he stayed not his hand this evil could not be turned aside.

label: he | take | counsel
sentence:
When Afrasiyab heard this message, he took counsel with Gersiwaz, and he said-
"If I cease from warring against Saiawosh surely none of these things can come about.

label: it | beseemeth | i
sentence:
It
beseemeth me to seek after peace.

label: i | send | silver
sentence:
I will send therefore silver and jewels and rich gifts
unto Saiawosh, and will bind up with gold the eye of war.

label: he | bid | ∅
sentence:
"
So he bade Gersiwaz take from his treasures rich brocades of Roum, and jewels of price,
and bear them across the Jihun to the camp of Saiawosh.

label: he | send | message
sentence:
And he sent a message unto him,
saying-
"The world is disturbed since the days of Silim and Tur, the valiant, since the times of Irij,
who was killed unjustly.

label: ∅ | let | ∅
sentence:
But now, let us forget these things, let us conclude an alliance
together, and let peace reign in our borders.

label: gersiwaz | do | bade
sentence:
And Gersiwaz did as Afrasiyab bade, and he went forth, and a train of camels bearing
rich presents followed after him.

label: he | march | ∅
sentence:
And he marched till he came within the tents of
Saiawosh.

label: king | marvel | thereat
sentence:
Now when he had delivered his message unto Saiawosh, the young King marvelled
thereat; and he took counsel with Rostam how they should act, for he trusted not in the
words of Afrasiyab, and he deemed that poison was hidden under these flowers.

label: rostam | counsel | he
sentence:
And
Rostam counselled him that they should entertain Gersiwaz the space of seven days, and
that joy and feasting should resound throughout the camp, and in the mean season they
would ponder their deeds.

label: it | do | ∅
sentence:
And it was done as Rostam said, and the sounds of revelry
were abroad, and Gersiwaz rejoiced in the presence of Saiawosh.

label: gersiwaz | present | himself
sentence:
But on the eighth day
Gersiwaz presented himself before Saiawosh in audience, and demanded a reply.

label: saiawosh | ponder | message
sentence:
And
Saiawosh said-
"We have pondered thy message, and we yield to thy request, for we desire not bloodshed
but peace.

label: we | desire | thee
sentence:
Yet since it behoveth us to know that poison be not hidden under thy words,
we desire of thee that thou send over to us as hostages an hundred chosen men of Turan,
allied unto Afrasiyab by blood, that we may guard them as a pledge of thy words.

label: he | send | it
sentence:
"
When Gersiwaz heard this answer, he sent it unto Afrasiyab by a messenger quick as the
wind.

label: saiawosh | deny | belief
sentence:
And Afrasiyab, when he heard it, was troubled, for he said-
"If I give way to this demand I bereave the land of its choicest warriors; yet if I refuse,
Saiawosh will deny belief unto my words, and the evils foretold will fall upon me.

label: he | choose | ∅
sentence:
"
So he chose out from among his army men allied to him by blood, and he sent them forth
unto Saiawosh.

label: he | cause | ∅
sentence:
Then he caused the trumpets to sound, and retreated with his army unto
Turan, and restored unto Iran the lands he had seized.

label: he | suffer | gersiwaz
sentence:
Now when Rostam beheld the warriors, and that Afrasiyab had spoken that which was
true, he suffered Gersiwaz to depart; and he held counsel with Saiawosh how they should
acquaint Kai Kawous with that which was come to pass, for Saiawosh said-
"If Kai Kawous desire vengeance rather than peace, he will be angered and commit a
deed of folly.

label: who | bear | he
sentence: Who shall bear unto him these tidings?"

label: rostam | say | ∅
sentence:
And Rostam said, "Suffer that I go forth to tell them unto Kai Kawous, for verily he will
listen unto that which I shall speak, and honour will fall upon Saiawosh for this
adventure.

label: rostam | go | king
sentence:
"
Wherefore Rostam went before the King, and told him they had conquered Afrasiyab,
and how he was become afraid, and how there was concluded a peace between them.

label: he | vaunt | wisdom
sentence:
And
he vaunted the wisdom of Saiawosh that was quick to act and quick to refrain, and he
craved the Shah to confirm what they had done.

label: kawous | anger | ∅
sentence:
But Kai Kawous was angered when he
heard it, and he said that Saiawosh had done like to an infant.

label: he | load | reproach
sentence:
And he loaded reproaches
upon Rostam, and said that his counsels were vile, and he sware that he would be
avenged upon Turan.

label: he | recall | all
sentence:
Then he recalled all they had suffered in the days that were past at
the hands of Afrasiyab, and he said the tree of vengeance could not be uprooted.

label: he | desire | rostam
sentence:
And he
desired Rostam that he turn him back unto Balkh, and say unto Saiawosh that he should
destroy these hostages of Turan, and that he should fall again upon Afrasiyab, nor cease
from fighting.

label: rostam | open | mouth
sentence:
But Rostam, when he had heard him to an end, opened his mouth and said
unto the Shah-
"O King, listen to my voice, and do not that which is evil!

label: i | say | thee
sentence:
Verily I say unto thee that
Saiawosh will not break his oath unto Afrasiyab, neither will he destroy these men of
Turan that were delivered into his hands."

label: kawous | hear | speech
sentence:
When Kai Kawous heard his speech his anger was kindled, and he upbraided Rostam,
and said that his evil counsels had caused Saiawosh to swerve from the straight path; and
he taunted him and bade him go back unto Seistan, and he said that Toos should go forth
as Pehliva unto his son.

label: rostam | anger | ∅
sentence:
Then Rostam too was angered, and he gave back the reproaches
of the Shah, and he turned him and quitted the courts and sped him back unto his
kingdom.

label: kawous | send | toos
sentence:
But Kai Kawous sent Toos unto the army at his borders, and he bade him speak
his desires unto Saiawosh his son.

label: ∅ | be | ∅
sentence:
Now Saiawosh, when he learned what was come about, was sore discomfited, and he
pondered how he should act.

label: i | come | ormuzd
sentence:
For he said, "How can I come before Ormuzd if I depart
from mine oath?

label: i | see | i
sentence: Yet, however I shall act, I see around me but perdition.

label: he | call | bahram
sentence:
"
Then he called for Bahram and Zengueh, and confided to them his troubles.

label: he | say | ∅
sentence:
And he said
how that Kai Kawous was a king who knew not good from evil, and how he had
accomplished that wherefore the army went forth, yet how the Shah desired that
vengeance should not cease.

label: he | said- | ∅
sentence:
And he said-
"If I listen to the commands of the King, I do that which is evil; yet if I listen not, surely
he will destroy me.

label: i | send | man
sentence:
Wherefore I will send back unto Afrasiyab the men he hath placed
within my hands, and then hide me from sight.

label: he | send | zengueh
sentence:
"
Then he sent Zengueh before Afrasiyab with a writing.

label: he | tell | all
sentence:
And he told therein all that was
come about, and how that discord was sprouted out of their peace.

label: he | recall | afrasiyab
sentence:
And he recalled unto
Afrasiyab how he had not broken their treaty though Kai Kawous had bidden him do it,
and he said how he could not return unto the King his father.

label: he | pray | afrasiyab
sentence:
Then he prayed Afrasiyab
that he would make a passage for him through his dominions, that he might hide him
wheresoever God desired.

label: i | seek | spot
sentence:
For he said-
"I seek a spot where my name shall be lost unto Kai Kawous, and where I may not know
of his woeful deeds.

label: zengueh | set | ∅
sentence:
"
And Zengueh set forth and did as Saiawosh desired, and he took with him the hundred
men of Turan, and all the gold and jewels that Afrasiyab had sent.

label: he | come | gate
sentence:
And when he was
come within the gates Afrasiyab received him right kindly, but when he had heard his
message he was downcast in his spirit.

label: he | call | piran
sentence:
Then he called for Piran, the leader of his hosts,
and he took counsel with him how he should act.

label: piran | live | ∅
sentence:
And Piran said-
"O King, live for ever!

label: ∅ | be | road
sentence: There is but one road open unto thee.

label: ∅ | be | prince
sentence:
For this Prince is noble, and
he hath done that which is right, for he would not give ear unto the evil designs of Kai
Kawous, his father.

label: he | mount | throne
sentence:
Wherefore I counsel thee, receive him within thy courts, and give
unto him a daughter in marriage, and let him be to thee a son; for verily, when Kai
Kawous shall die, he will mount upon the throne of Iran, and thus may the hate of old be
quenched in love.

label: afrasiyab | know | ∅
sentence:
"
Now Afrasiyab, when he had listened to the words of Piran, knew that they were good.

label: he | send | scribe
sentence: So he sent for a scribe, and dictated a writing unto Saiawosh.

label: he | say | he
sentence:
And he said unto him how
the land was open to receive him, and how he would be to him a father, and how he
should find in Turan the love that was denied of Kai Kawous.

label: i | suffer | suspicion
sentence:
And he said-
"I will demand of thee nought but what is good, neither will I suffer suspicion against
thee to enter my soul.

label: he | seal | letter
sentence:
"
Then he sealed the letter with his royal seal, and gave it unto Zengueh the messenger, and
bade him depart there with speed.

label: saiawosh | be | ∅
sentence:
And Saiawosh, when he had read it, was glad, and yet
he was also troubled in his spirit, for his heart was sore because he was forced to make a
friend of the foe of his land.

label: he | see | ∅
sentence: Yet he saw that it could in nowise be altered.

label: he | write | letter
sentence:
So he wrote a
letter to Kai Kawous, and he told him therein how it seemed that he could not do that
which was right in his eyes, and he recalled unto him the troubles that were come upon
him from Sudaveh, and he said how he could not break an oath he had made.

label: he | confide | writing
sentence:
Then he
confided this writing unto Bahram, and he bade him take the lead of the army till that
Toos should be come forth from Iran.

label: he | depart | they
sentence:
And when he had chosen out an hundred warriors
of renown from out the host, he departed with them across the border.

label: he | confound | ∅
sentence:
Now when Toos arrived and learned what was come to pass, he was confounded; and
when tidings thereof reached Kai Kawous, he was struck down with dismay.

label: he | cry | afrasiyab
sentence:
He cried out
against Afrasiyab, and against Saiawosh his son, and his anger was kindled.

label: he | refrain | combat
sentence:
Yet he
refrained from combat, and his mouth was silent of war.

label: saiawosh | come | meantime
sentence:
In the meantime Saiawosh was come into Turan, and all the land had decked itself to do
him honour.

label: piran | come | ∅
sentence:
And Piran came forth to greet him, and there followed after him elephants,
white of hue, richly caparisoned, laden with gifts.

label: he | pour | these
sentence:
And these he poured before Saiawosh,
and gave him welcome.

label: he | tell | he
sentence:
And he told him how Afrasiyab yearned to look upon his face,
and he said-
"Turn thee in amity unto the King, and let not thy mind be troubled concerning that
which thou hast heard about him.

label: ∅ | hath | fame
sentence:
For Afrasiyab hath an ill fame, but he deserveth it not,
for he is good."

label: piran | lead | saiawosh
sentence: Then Piran led Saiawosh before Afrasiyab.

label: he | rejoice | strength
sentence:
And when Afrasiyab saw him, he rejoiced at
his strength and his beauty, and his heart went out towards him, and he embraced him,
and spake, saying-
"The evil that hath disturbed the world is quieted, and the lamb and the leopard can feed
together, for now is there friendship between our lands.

label: he | call | blessing
sentence:
"
Then he called down blessings upon the head of Saiawosh, and he took him by the hand
and seated him beside him on the throne.

label: he | turn | piran
sentence:
And he turned to Piran, and said-
"Kai Kawous is a man void of sense, or surely he would not suffer a son like unto this to
depart from out his sight."

label: afrasiyab | cease | ∅
sentence:
And Afrasiyab could not cease from gazing upon Saiawosh, and all that he had he placed
it at his command.

label: he | give | palace
sentence:
He gave to him a palace, and rich brocades, and jewels and gold past
the counting; and he prepared for him a feast, and there were played the games of skill,
and Saiawosh showed his prowess before Afrasiyab.

label: sight | become | light
sentence:
And the sight of Saiawosh became a
light to the eyes of the King of Turan and a joy unto his heart, and he loved him like to a
father.

label: saiawosh | abode | court
sentence:
And Saiawosh abode within his courts many days, and in gladness and in sorrow,
in gaiety or in sadness, Afrasiyab would have none other about him.

label: ∅ | roll | moon
sentence:
And in this wise there rolled twelve moons over their
heads, and in the end Saiawosh took unto himself to wife the daughter of Piran the
Pehliva.

label: heaven | revolve | head
sentence:
And yet again the heavens revolved above his head, and he continued to abide
within the house of Afrasiyab.

label: piran | give | counsel
sentence:
Then Piran gave counsel unto Saiawosh that he should ask
of Afrasiyab the hand of his daughter to wife.

label: home | be | said-
sentence:
For he said-
"Thy home is now in Turan, wherefore it behoveth thee to establish thy might; and if
Afrasiyab be thy father indeed, there can no hurt come near to thee.

label: he | bind | enmity
sentence:
And peradventure, if
a son be born unto thee of the daughter of Afrasiyab, he will bind up for ever the enmity
of the lands.

label: saiawosh | listen | counsel
sentence:
"
And Saiawosh listened to the counsel of Piran, for he knew that it was good, and he asked
the hand of Ferangis of her father, and Afrasiyab gave it to him with great joy.

label: feast | make | bridal
sentence:
Then a
mighty feast was made for the bridal, and Afrasiyab poured gifts upon Saiawosh past the
telling, and he bestowed on him a kingdom and a throne, and he blessed him as his son;
and when at length he suffered him to go forth unto his realm, he sorrowed sore at his
loss.

label: saiawosh | abide | province
sentence:
Now the space of one year did Saiawosh abide in his province, and at the end thereof,
when he had visited its breadth, he builded for himself a city in the midst.

label: he | name | it
sentence:
And he named
it Gangdis, and it was a place of beauty, such as the world hath not seen the like.

label: saiawosh | build | house
sentence:
And
Saiawosh built houses and planted trees without number, and he also caused an open
space to be made wherein men could rejoice in the game of ball.

label: he | be | ∅
sentence:
And he was glad in the
possession of this city, and all men around him rejoiced, and the earth was the happier for
his presence, and there was no cloud upon the heaven of his life.

label: mubid | tell | he
sentence:
Yet the Mubids told
unto him that Gangdis would lead to his ill-fortune, and Saiawosh was afflicted thereat.

label: he | put | word
sentence:
But when a little time was sped and he beheld no evil, he put from him their words, and
he rejoiced in the time that was; and he was glad in the house of his women, and he put
his trust in Afrasiyab.

label: it | accomplish | ∅
sentence: But that which is written in the stars, surely it must be accomplished!

label: it | come | year
sentence:
So it came about
after many years that Gersiwaz was jealous of the love which Afrasiyab his brother bare
unto Saiawosh, and of the power that was his; and he pondered in his heart how he might
destroy him.

label: he | come | afrasiyab
sentence:
Then he came before Afrasiyab, and prayed the King that he would suffer
him to go forth and visit the city that Saiawosh had builded, whereof the mouths of men
ran over in praises.

label: afrasiyab | grant | request
sentence:
And Afrasiyab granted his request, and bade him bear words of love
unto Saiawosh his son.

label: gersiwaz | speed | city
sentence:
So Gersiwaz sped forth unto the city of Gangdis, and the master
thereof received him kindly, and asked him tidings of the King.

label: he | feast | house
sentence:
And he feasted him many
days within his house, and he showed freely unto him all that was his; and when he
departed he heaped gifts upon his head, for he knew not that Gersiwaz came in enmity
unto him, and that these things but fanned his envy.

label: king | question | he
sentence:
Now when Gersiwaz returned unto Afrasiyab, the King questioned him concerning his
darling.

label: gersiwaz | answer | ∅
sentence:
Then Gersiwaz answered and said-
"O King, he is no longer the man whom thou knewest.

label: spirit | be | ∅
sentence:
His spirit is uplifted in pride of
might, and his heart goeth out towards Iran.

label: i | hide | grief
sentence:
And but that I should make my name to be
infamous unto the nations, I would have hidden from thee this grief.

label: it | behoveth | i
sentence:
But it behoveth me
to tell unto thee that which I have seen and which mine ears have heard.

label: it | make | known
sentence:
For it hath been
made known unto me that Saiawosh is in treaty with his father, and that they seek to
destroy thee utterly.

label: he | refuse | countenance
sentence:
"
When Afrasiyab heard these words he would not let them take root in his spirit, yet he
could not refuse countenance to the testimony of his brother.

label: he | be | ∅
sentence:
And he was sad, and spake
not, and Gersiwaz knew not whether the seeds he had strewn had taken root.

label: he | come | king
sentence:
So when a
few days were gone by he came again before the King and repeated unto him the charges
that he had made, and he urged him to act, and suffer not Turan to be disgraced.

label: afrasiyab | catch | mesh
sentence:
Then
Afrasiyab was caught in the meshes of the net that Gersiwaz had spread.

label: he | bid | ∅
sentence:
And he bade
Gersiwaz go forth and summon Saiawosh unto his courts, and invite him to bring the
daughter of Afrasiyab to feast with her father.

label: gersiwaz | speed | gladness
sentence:
And Gersiwaz sped forth with gladness,
and delivered the message of Afrasiyab unto the young King.

label: i | be | ∅
sentence:
Then Saiawosh said-
"I am ready to do the will of Afrasiyab, and the bridle of my horse is tied unto thy
charger."

label: gersiwaz | think | he
sentence:
Then Gersiwaz thought within him, "If Saiawosh come into the presence of Afrasiyab,
his courage and open spirit will give the lie unto my words."

label: he | feign | saiawosh
sentence:
So he feigned before Saiawosh a great sorrow, and when the King questioned him thereof
he consented to pour out before him the griefs of his spirit.

label: he | say | he
sentence:
And he said to him how that
he loved him tenderly, and how he was in sorrow for his sake, because that the ear of
Afrasiyab had been poisoned against him, and he counselled him that he should not seek
the courts of the King.

label: he | said- | ∅
sentence:
And he said-
"Suffer me to return alone, and I will soften the heart of Afrasiyab towards thee; and
when he shall be returned unto a right spirit, I will summon thee forth unto his house."

label: saiawosh | listen | word
sentence:
Now Saiawosh, who was true and void of guile, listened unto these words, for he knew
not that they were false.

label: he | send | word
sentence:
So he sent words of greeting and of excuse unto Afrasiyab, and
he said that he could not quit the chamber of Ferangis, for she was sick and chained unto
her couch.

label: gersiwaz | ride | ∅
sentence:
And Gersiwaz rode forth bearing the letter, and he sware unto Saiawosh that
he would cement the peace that was broken.

label: he | deliver | writing
sentence:
But when he came unto Afrasiyab he
delivered not the writing, but spake evil things of Saiawosh, and maligned him.

label: he | feed | anger
sentence:
And he
fed the anger of Afrasiyab, until the King commanded that the army be led forth to go
against Saiawosh his friend, and he took the lead thereof himself.

label: gersiwaz | send | envoy
sentence:
Now when the men of Turan came nigh unto the city that Saiawosh had builded,
Gersiwaz sent an envoy unto Saiawosh, saying-
"Flee, I counsel thee, for my words have availed nought, and Afrasiyab cometh forth in
enmity against thee.

label: he | be | downcast
sentence:
"
When Saiawosh learned this he was sore downcast in his spirit, and he went unto
Ferangis and charged her how she should act when he should be fallen by the hands of
Afrasiyab, for he held it vile to go forth in combat with one who had been to him a father.

label: he | make | ∅
sentence: So he made ready his house for death.

label: he | press | head
sentence:
Now when he came to his steed of battle he
pressed its head unto his breast, and he wept over it and spake into its ear.

label: ∅ | listen | ∅
sentence:
And he said-
"Listen, O my horse, and be brave and prudent; neither attach thyself unto any man until
the day that Kai Khosrau, my son, shall arise to avenge me.

label: ∅ | receive | saddle
sentence:
From him alone receive the
saddle and the rein.

label: he | bid | man
sentence:
"
Then he bade the men of Iran that were about him go back unto their land, and when all
was ready he went forth beyond the gates.

label: he | hop | ∅
sentence:
But even yet he hoped to turn from him the
suspicions of Afrasiyab, and he would not suffer his men to offer combat unto the men of
Turan.

label: he | go | afrasiyab
sentence:
So he went before Afrasiyab, and questioned him wherefore he was come out in
anger against him.

label: gersiwaz | suffer | afrasiyab
sentence:
Now Gersiwaz suffered not Afrasiyab to reply, but heaped reproaches
upon Saiawosh, and said that he had received him vilely, and that he had slandered his
benefactor.

label: saiawosh | confound | ∅
sentence:
And Saiawosh, when he had listened, was confounded, and in vain did he
strive to bear down the upbraiding of his foe.

label: ∅ | anger | heart
sentence:
For the heart of Afrasiyab was angered yet
the more, now that his eyes rested yet again upon the face of Saiawosh, whom he loved,
because he deemed that he must give credit unto the words of his brother, and because
distrust of Iran was graven in his soul.

label: he | harden | himself
sentence:
So he hardened himself against the speech of
Saiawosh, and he bade the army fall upon his beloved.

label: saiawosh | remember | oath
sentence:
But Saiawosh remembered his
oath, and he stretched not forth his hand against Afrasiyab, neither did he defend himself
from the assaults of his men, and he bade the warriors that were with him that they
unsheathe not the sword.

label: they | mow | ∅
sentence:
So speedily were they mown down, and their bodies lay round
about Saiawosh their King.

label: ∅ | stretch | hand
sentence:
And when all were slain a knight stretched out his hand
against Saiawosh, yet he slew him not, but bound him with cords, and led him before
Afrasiyab the King.

label: afrasiyab | command | ∅
sentence:
And Afrasiyab commanded that Saiawosh be led forth into a desert
place, and that his head be severed from off his trunk.

label: army | murmur | ∅
sentence:
Now the army murmured when
they heard this command, and beheld the beauty of Saiawosh and his face of truth, and
there stepped forth one from among the nobles to plead for him.

label: gersiwaz | suffer | heart
sentence:
But Gersiwaz would not
suffer the heart of Afrasiyab to be softened.

label: ferangis | come | house
sentence:
Now while Gersiwaz yet spake evil of the young King, there came forth from the house
of the women Ferangis, the daughter of Afrasiyab, and she demanded audience of her
father.

label: she | force | herself
sentence:
And when he would have denied it, she forced herself into his presence, and she
pleaded for her lord, and she sware that evil tongues had maligned him, and she entreated
of her father that he would not destroy the joy he had given to her.

label: ∅ | listen | ∅
sentence:
And she said-
"Listen, O King!

label: ∅ | deliver | land
sentence:
Deliver
not by thy folly the land of Turan unto the winds, and remember the deeds that have been
done of Iran in the days that are gone by.

label: avenger | arise | midst
sentence:
An avenger will arise from out the midst of the
Kaianides.

label: thou | recall | counsel
sentence: Mayest thou never recall my counsel too late.

label: world | grow | eye
sentence:
"
But the world grew dark before the eyes of Afrasiyab with anger.

label: which | be | ∅
sentence:
And he spake, and said-
"Go hence, and trouble not again my face; for how canst thou judge of that which is
right?

label: he | command | ∅
sentence:
"
Then he commanded that she should be bound, and cast into a dungeon.

label: gersiwaz | deem | ∅
sentence: Now Gersiwaz, when he beheld the anger of the King, deemed that the time was ripe.

label: he | give | sign
sentence:
He
therefore gave a sign unto the men that held Saiawosh in bondage, and desired that they
should slay him.

label: they | drag | he
sentence:
And by the hairs of his head they dragged him unto a desert place, and
the sword of Gersiwaz was planted in the breast of the royal cedar.

label: storm | arise | earth
sentence:
But when it was done,
and they had severed the head from the trunk, a mighty storm arose over the earth, and
the heavens were darkened.

label: they | tremble | ∅
sentence:
Then they trembled and were sore afraid, and repented them
of their deed.

label: clamour | arise | house
sentence:
And clamour arose in the house of Saiawosh, and the cries of Ferangis
reached even unto Afrasiyab her father.

label: king | command | ∅
sentence:
Then the King commanded that she should be
killed also.

label: i | counsel | thee
sentence:
Shed not, I counsel thee, the blood of yet
another innocent.

label: i | pray | confide
sentence:
But if thou desire to look no more upon Ferangis, I pray thee confide
her unto me, that she may be to me a daughter in my house, and I will guard her from
sorrow.

label: ∅ | do | that
sentence:
"
Then Afrasiyab said-
"Do that which seemeth best in thy sight.

label: he | be | ∅
sentence:
"
And he was glad in his heart, for he desired not to look upon the face that should recall to
him the friend that he had loved.

label: piran | take | ferangis
sentence:
So Piran took Ferangis unto his house beyond the
mountains, and Afrasiyab returned unto his courts.

label: king | be | spirit
sentence:
But the King was sorrowful in his
spirit and unquiet in his heart, and he could not cease from thinking of Saiawosh, and he
repented of that which he had done.
